summarization.a: The figure compares multiple infrared difference spectra of a B2O3 ALD film recorded after the first and ninth HF exposures at 150 °C, together with an inverted reference spectrum. The progressive loss of the B–O stretching band and the appearance of B–F related features indicate systematic chemical modification and fluorination of the B2O3 film with repeated HF exposure.

data_extraction.a:
| Wavenumber region (cm⁻¹) | Assignment | Trend across spectra |
|--------------------------|------------|----------------------|
| ~1400–1600 | B–O stretch | Strong in B2O3 ALD, progressively reduced after repeated HF exposure |
| ~1350 | B–F mode | Appears after HF exposure and strengthens with cycling |
| ~800–900 | Framework modes | Minor changes between spectra |

a.question: What spectral changes indicate chemical modification of the B2O3 film during HF exposure?
a.answer: The decrease of the B–O stretching band shows that B–O bonds are being removed or altered during HF exposure. At the same time, new features associated with B–F bonding appear, indicating fluorination of the surface as the reaction proceeds.

a.question: Which vibrational feature is associated with the B–O stretch?
a.answer: The band in the ~1400–1600 cm⁻¹ region.

a.question: How does the spectrum after the ninth HF exposure differ from the first?
a.answer: The ninth HF exposure shows a weaker B–O stretch and more pronounced B–F related features.

a.question: Does repeated HF exposure significantly alter the chemical structure of the B2O3 ALD film?
